transcript: Yeah, is Roger there? Hey, Roger?
Hi, yeah, my name’s Rocco. I just live a couple places down from you. Ah, how you doin’ today?
Do you even know where I live?

Yeah, I got your number from Big Red, our other neighbor. I’m just calling everyone in our street ‘cause I’m gonna be letting off some holograms outside. And I just kinda wanted to notify you and let you know what I’ll be doing, if you look outside. Maybe just stay inside for a couple of hours, if that’s okay with you?—
And where—? And where do you live?
Uh, same street as you, sir. He—he told me ta—
Why don’t you say the street?
I just moved here from Crested Butte, Colorado—
I’m real new—like three days ago.
Okay. Yeah, I’m not. I’ve been here a long time and—
…a Colorado number callin’ tellin’ me not to come out of my house? Le—let’s make it real plain and simple. Somebody comes to my house? They may not survive the encounter.
Well it’s—it’s just an avatar. It’s not a real person.
Then—then it may survive the encounter if it’s not a real person. But a real person probably wouldn’t survive the encounter comin’ here—
Whadda you mean there? [emergency siren] Well I’ve got one avatar of, like, these shrunken heads. It just looks like they’re flyin’ up ’n down—
So that’s what I’ll be doing.
I don’t know who the hell you— I don’t know who the hell you are and I don’t know how the hell you got my number, but I’m tellin’ ya— I’m tellin’ ya—
From Big Red, like I said.
Come to my house and we’ll play. Really not in the mood to go ‘round and ‘round with ya, bud.
Roger, I just think we got off on the wrong foot, here, okay?—
Oh, I guarantee you we did. So you do you on your property and I’ll do me on my property. If that’s a problem, we’ll deal with it then.
Listen, I just got a hologram of a kangaroo—
Buddy, you continue to call me and you’re gonna end up in jail.
You wanna get into it, guy? ‘Cause I can stop by—
I tried to tell ya fair ’n square. I got a kangaroo hologram movin’ around. It’s perfectly safe—

Okay. Like I said: you do you on your property, m’kay? Anybody comes to my property and they continue to say that they wanna go ‘round and ‘round? My stuff isn’t holograms. Now, I’m at work. If you call—
Oh, buddy, I’ll go ‘round and ‘round right now!
If you wanna get wild let’s go, bucko!
This is Rocco. I just moved in down the street. How ya doin’?

Yeah. Hey, I was given your number by the Superintendent; he’s a friend of mine over there. He’s got everybody’s number. But hey, listen! I’m gonna be outside settin’ off some holograms today and I just wanted to let ya know what I’ll be doing if you see me outside. Maybe you wanna stay inside for the next few hours, if that’s okay with you? [sniffs] [sfx, gust of wind]—
What do you—? What do you mean by that? I’m not sure talking about, here.
Oh! Like I have a hologram of a human cannonball. It looks like he’s just flyin’ down the street. [continual gusts] it’s very realistic! Very lifelike! Um—
O—okay and who gave you number?
…there’s a chance the images might travel through your window. They can’t come through walls, but it may, periodically, come through the window. But I’ll try to keep that to a minimum—
…okay? So you’ll stay inside for a few hours, then? [wind]
I mean stay away from home for a few hours, then, or…? [delay] [wind]
I mean—I mean, if I need to go home, I’mma go home. Uh— Is there anything preventing me from getting in the house?
Just a human cannonball flyin’ like wild down the street!
…yeah, I guess I’ll see what you’re talkin’ ‘bout when I see it. I don’t— I’m not really sure. I don’t understand what’s goin’ on, but I’mma be home when I wanna go home, alright?—
I might plug into your outlet outside, too, if that’s okay. Alright?
I’m not approvin’ you to plug into my condo’s outlet; not doin’ it. You can plug into the buildin’s outlet. But my condo? I’m not payin’ for yo shit!
It’s only for a few hours, dawg! [exhales]
Yeah, so it’s a “few hours” that I can’t go to my condo and then I gotta pay for your electricity? No! I’m not doin’ that!
Lend me some skin, blood! Come on, blood! [delay] [exhales]
I don’t even know— How did you get my number? Who is this “Superintendent” that gave you my number?—
Oh! C’mon, Jack! Slap me somethin’! [chuckles]
I’ve never had a phone call like this. I don’t know what this is about. [laughs]
I need to work on my holograms outside; that’s all there is to it!—
Well h— Okay! Work on your holograms, man! I don’t—I mean—I don’t know how this affects me. I’m gonna get to my house if I need to get to my house.
What if a human cannonball’s flyin’ down the street? Now how you gon’ like that? [delay]
Dude, what the fuck is this? Who are you, dude?
Rocco. I got your number from the Superintendent, like I said. [wind]
Okay! Gee, do what you wanna do! I’m gonna get to my house if I need to get to my house. Like, have fun. I don’t care what you’re doin’, but I’mma get to my house.
Yeah, this is Rocco. I just live down yonder from you. I got your number from Rota the mail-lady. How are ya today?
I’m doin’ okay. What can I do for you?
Good, yeah. I’m just calling everybody on the street—
…because I’m gonna be, ah, setting off some holograms outside—out in front of the house. And I just wanted to—
…let ya know what I’ll be doing. Yeah—
So mayb—maybe stay inside for a while—if that’s okay?—for two, three hours?
Well yeah, just to avoid any confusion. Like, I have a hologram of, like, a human cannonball—looks like he’s just flyin’ down the street. And I’m kinda a pioneer with avatars and holograms [exhales]
Okay. I’m just a little confused as to what exactly is going on. I’m a little confused. [chuckles] I’m—

Well there’s a chance that the hologram may, ah, travel through your window. It can’t go through walls or anything but it may appear through your window inside your place. But I’ll try to keep that to a minimum. If that were—
I mean, do you have permission to do this and, like, should I be concerned? I’m—I’m confused. [inhales & clears throat]
Yeah, you probably wanna stay indoors! Uh-huh! [telephone ringing] You think—
Do you have permission for this? LIke, I don’t understand—
Oh yeah! I talked to the mail-lady about it and everything about it. She gave me your number. I’m callin’ everybody on the street. [exhales]
I’m confused as to why the mail-lady has any jurisdiction over when I can and can’t leave my house.

Well, there’s another avatar I have of, like, a kangaroo; it looks like he’s just bouncin’ like wild! [delay] Down the street. And so I’m just lettin’ ya know just to give ya a heads-up. So that’s why I said best, maybe, to stay inside—
Okay! I’m— Like I said, do you have legal right to do this? I don’t know who you are. I don’t know why you have my phone number, honestly; that’s a little disconcerting—
From the mail lady. I’m Rocco!—
I don’t care [chuckles] who you got it from! I don’t know who this “mail lady” is! I don’t know why—
…the mail lady has my phone number. I don’t know who “Rota” is. I don’t care who she is—
The mail-person has no right to give out my personal information like my phone number and my name! I don’t understand who you are! I’m upset, honestly, over this whole conversation [huffs]
Well, just look out and maybe stay indoors the next few days!
I might—I might have to call somebody to see what the hell’s goin’ on. Okay?—
Call who? What do you mean?—
Listen. I’m a pioneer. I’m just tryin’ to—
Tha— I don’t like that you have my contact information and my name. I don’t appreciate it. I don’t care where you got it from. I don’t like it. I don’t appreciate it. Okay? [clears throat]
I’m callin’ everybody on the street, guy, just to give ya a heads-up—
I’m doin’ you a favor here!
[nervous huffing] You’re doing me a favor? [laughs] Listen, dude [laughs] do your thing and we’ll see, okay?—
Hey, guy, I got holograms out the yingyang over here. I have one of a matador—
…with, like, a real defiant stance; that’s the icing on the cake, on that one. Ya feel me?—
I don’t care what you have! [laughs] Enjoy!—
So just stay inside and we’ll be good to go. Understand?

No, I don’t. I don’t unders—don’t understand why you have my contact infor— I still— It still is upsetting to me that somebody I don’t know, who I have no reason to have my contact information, has my contact information. The second part that’s upsetting is that you got it from my mail-lady who shouldn’t be giving out my information—
Hercules. And I’ll try to keep it from going—
through your window! I’ll do my best!
This is a big deal, guy!
Yeah, Marvin, please. Is Marvin there?
Hi, this is Rocco. I’m just, uh, kinda calling everyone on the street. I’m gonna be lettin’ off some, ah—
…let you know what I’ll be doing, okay?—
What street do you live on?
I moved to Flatbush from Temecula, California about—
[laughs] Where’d you get my number from?
Uh, from Big Red, our other neighbor; said to call him and call the other lady—
I just got holograms poppin’ off. I just wanted ta let ya know; that’s all! I got one of, like, a kangaroo except it looks like he’s in a—
’S that funny to you or somethin’?
I’ve got one that looks like a matador except there’s all these stalagmites [sfx]
